req.title: Procedure record (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall record a Procedure tied to an Encounter. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-061 requires that a Procedure is correctly tied to an open Encounter, with explicit negative paths for missing encounter references, permission denial, missing mandatory fields, and conflicting state transitions. The suite covers: (1) a valid Procedure POST tied to an open Encounter (EP positive), (2) a POST with a missing encounter reference yielding 422 (NEG), (3) a POST where the subject identifier is exactly at the minimum required length boundary (BVA), (4) a POST by a user lacking clinician permissions yielding 401/403 (NEG/DT), and (5) a POST to a closed/completed Encounter representing a conflicting state transition yielding 422/409 (NEG, heuristic STC is not applicable to negative kind — corrected to NEG). The violation was test[4] having kind=negative but heuristic=STC; STC is reserved for positive state-transition coverage. The closed-encounter conflict test is correctly a negative test and its heuristic is updated to NEG to satisfy the rule.

tests.name: test_create_procedure_with_valid_encounter
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
A valid open Encounter resource exists with id <open_encounter_id>
actions: Build a minimal valid Procedure payload referencing the open Encounter
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body encounter.reference matches the submitted encounter id
trace: R-HLT-061: encounter open or referenced precondition
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"

def test_create_procedure_with_valid_encounter(clinician_token, open_encounter_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Procedure",
        "status": "completed",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "encounter": {"reference": f"Encounter/{open_encounter_id}"},
        "code": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct", "code": "71388002"}]
        }
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Procedure", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Procedure"
    enc_ref = body.get("encounter", {}).get("reference", "")
    assert open_encounter_id in enc_ref

tests.name: test_create_procedure_missing_encounter_returns_422
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
No Encounter resource with id 'nonexistent-enc-999' exists
actions: Build a Procedure payload with encounter.reference pointing to a non-existent Encounter
Capture HTTP response status and OperationOutcome body
expected: OperationOutcome issue describes missing or invalid encounter reference
trace: R-HLT-061: error pathway 422 when encounter missing

tests.name: test_create_procedure_subject_reference_at_minimum_length
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
A valid open Encounter resource exists with id <open_encounter_id>
Platform minimum allowed Patient id length is 1 character
actions: Build a Procedure payload where subject.reference uses a single-character Patient id ('Patient/1')
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 201 Created or 422 per platform id policy (documented)
If 422: OperationOutcome issue describes subject id constraint
trace: R-HLT-061: missing mandatory elements negative path (boundary of validity)

tests.name: test_create_procedure_unauthorized_user_denied
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
A valid open Encounter resource exists with id <open_encounter_id>
Auth token <non_clinician_token> belongs to a user without clinician role
actions: Build a valid Procedure payload referencing the open Encounter

tests.name: test_create_procedure_against_closed_encounter_rejected
preconditions: FHIR-lite service is running and reachable
An Encounter with status 'finished' exists with id <closed_encounter_id>
actions: Build a valid Procedure payload referencing the finished/closed Encounter
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: OperationOutcome issue describes conflicting encounter state or closed encounter rejection
trace: R-HLT-061: conflicting state transitions negative path
R-HLT-061: encounter open or referenced precondition violated
tests.executable:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"

def test_create_procedure_against_closed_encounter_rejected(
        clinician_token, closed_encounter_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Procedure",
        "status": "completed",
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-1"},
        "encounter": {"reference": f"Encounter/{closed_encounter_id}"},
        "code": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct", "code": "71388002"}]
        }
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Procedure", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (409, 422), (
        f"Expected 409 or 422 for closed encounter, got {resp.status_code}")
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert len(issues) > 0, "Expected at least one OperationOutcome issue for closed encounter conflict"
